groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Minimum product, customer, marketplace, and positioning information needed to create a valid first listing.

groups.title: Product Facts & Purchase Info
groups.description: Objective details that affect purchase decisions, product accuracy, compatibility, care, safety, and package expectations.

groups.title: Search & Positioning
groups.description: Keyword research, positioning preferences, brand tone, and wording restrictions without turning the form into an SEO questionnaire.

groups.title: Compliance, Variations & Sources
groups.description: Regulated claims, Seller Central category rules, product variations, existing listings, source material, and unusual requirements.

variables.label: Amazon Marketplace
variables.placeholder: Amazon.com US
variables.hint: Specify the marketplace and country for the listing.

help.what: Provide the amazon marketplace for this Amazon listing.
help.why: Marketplace context affects language, assumptions, limits, and category caution.
help.example: Good example: Amazon.com US
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented product facts, or claims the product cannot support.

variables.label: Output Language
variables.placeholder: English
variables.hint: Choose the language for the output.

help.what: Optional context for the output language field.
help.why: Listing copy should match the language used in the target marketplace.
help.example: Good example: English
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Product Name
variables.placeholder: Adjustable Bamboo Drawer Organizer
variables.hint: Enter the exact product name or working product name.

help.what: Provide the product name for this Amazon listing.
help.why: The title, bullets, and description need a stable product identity.
help.example: Good example: Adjustable Bamboo Drawer Organizer
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented product facts, or claims the product cannot support.

variables.label: Brand Name
variables.placeholder: Northside Home
variables.hint: Add the brand name if it should appear in the listing.

help.what: Optional context for the brand name field.
help.why: Brand context may affect title phrasing, store tone, and A+ section framing.
help.example: Good example: Northside Home
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Product Category
variables.placeholder: Kitchen drawer organizers
variables.hint: Describe the Amazon category or product type.

help.what: Provide the product category for this Amazon listing.
help.why: Category context affects feature emphasis, compliance caution, and buyer expectations.
help.example: Good example: Kitchen drawer organizers
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented product facts, or claims the product cannot support.

variables.label: Target Customer
variables.placeholder: Apartment renters and homeowners who want tidier kitchen drawers without custom cabinetry.
variables.hint: Describe the specific buyer and situation.

help.what: Provide the target customer for this Amazon listing.
help.why: Buyer context guides benefits, objections, use cases, and wording.
help.example: Good example: Apartment renters and homeowners who want tidier kitchen drawers without custom cabinetry.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, invented product facts, or claims the product cannot support.

variables.label: Verified Product Facts
variables.placeholder: Expandable bamboo organizer, 7 compartments, fits drawers from 13 to 20 inches wide, non-slip feet, no tools required.
variables.hint: Provide only confirmed product facts that may be used publicly.

help.what: Provide the verified product facts for this Amazon listing.
help.why: This is the factual source of truth for the listing.
help.example: Good example: Expandable bamboo organizer, 7 compartments, fits drawers from 13 to 20 inches wide, non-slip feet, no tools required.
help.avoid: Avoid future features, unverified results, ratings, awards, or claims not backed by product source material.

variables.label: Key Differentiators
variables.placeholder: Expandable width, smooth bamboo finish, removable dividers, and stable non-slip base.
variables.hint: Explain what is meaningfully different or useful about the product.

help.what: Optional context for the key differentiators field.
help.why: Differentiators help positioning without inventing superiority claims.
help.example: Good example: Expandable width, smooth bamboo finish, removable dividers, and stable non-slip base.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Primary Use Cases
variables.placeholder: Organizing cutlery, cooking tools, office supplies, and small household items.
variables.hint: List the main real-world uses for the product.

help.what: Optional context for the primary use cases field.
help.why: Use cases make the listing more concrete and buyer-focused.
help.example: Good example: Organizing cutlery, cooking tools, office supplies, and small household items.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Primary Listing Goal
variables.placeholder: Select listing goal
variables.hint: Choose the main optimization goal.

help.what: Optional context for the primary listing goal field.
help.why: The goal changes the balance between discovery, confidence, launch, and premium positioning.
help.example: Good example: Balanced conversion and discoverability
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Technical Specifications
variables.placeholder: Expands from 13 to 20 inches wide, 17 inches deep, 2 inches high.
variables.hint: Add measurable specs that affect purchase decisions.

help.what: Optional context for the technical specifications field.
help.why: Specifications prevent vague copy and reduce buyer uncertainty.
help.example: Good example: Expands from 13 to 20 inches wide, 17 inches deep, 2 inches high.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Materials or Ingredients
variables.placeholder: Bamboo body with silicone feet.
variables.hint: List materials, ingredients, finish, or composition.

help.what: Optional context for the materials or ingredients field.
help.why: Materials and ingredients can affect buyer trust, fit, care, and compliance.
help.example: Good example: Bamboo body with silicone feet.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Dimensions and Weight
variables.placeholder: 13 to 20 in W x 17 in D x 2 in H, 2.4 lb.
variables.hint: Provide dimensions, weight, size, or capacity.

help.what: Optional context for the dimensions and weight field.
help.why: Physical details often determine whether the product fits the buyer’s needs.
help.example: Good example: 13 to 20 in W x 17 in D x 2 in H, 2.4 lb.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: What Is Included
variables.placeholder: One expandable organizer and four removable dividers.
variables.hint: List exactly what the buyer receives.

help.what: Optional context for the what is included field.
help.why: Package contents reduce confusion and prevent implied extras.
help.example: Good example: One expandable organizer and four removable dividers.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Compatibility or Fit
variables.placeholder: Fits drawers at least 13 inches wide, 17 inches deep, and 2.25 inches tall.
variables.hint: Describe fit, compatibility, supported models, or exclusions.

help.what: Optional context for the compatibility or fit field.
help.why: Compatibility details prevent misleading universal-fit claims.
help.example: Good example: Fits drawers at least 13 inches wide, 17 inches deep, and 2.25 inches tall.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Care Instructions
variables.placeholder: Wipe clean with a damp cloth and dry immediately.
variables.hint: Add care, usage, maintenance, or storage instructions.

help.what: Optional context for the care instructions field.
help.why: Care instructions help set accurate expectations after purchase.
help.example: Good example: Wipe clean with a damp cloth and dry immediately.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Safety Warnings
variables.placeholder: Keep away from open flame and do not soak in water.
variables.hint: Add warnings, age limits, hazards, or safe-use notes.

help.what: Optional context for the safety warnings field.
help.why: Safety wording must not be invented or omitted when material.
help.example: Good example: Keep away from open flame and do not soak in water.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Warranty or Support
variables.placeholder: One-year limited warranty and email support.
variables.hint: Describe warranty, support, returns, or customer help details.

help.what: Optional context for the warranty or support field.
help.why: Support details can improve purchase confidence when confirmed.
help.example: Good example: One-year limited warranty and email support.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Primary Keywords
variables.placeholder: bamboo drawer organizer, expandable cutlery tray
variables.hint: Add the most important search terms from research.

help.what: Optional context for the primary keywords field.
help.why: Keywords guide discovery without forcing keyword stuffing.
help.example: Good example: bamboo drawer organizer, expandable cutlery tray
help.avoid: Avoid keyword stuffing, competitor brand terms, or irrelevant search terms.

variables.label: Secondary Keywords
variables.placeholder: kitchen drawer organizer, utensil organizer, silverware tray, office drawer organizer
variables.hint: Add supporting search terms, synonyms, and buyer language.

help.what: Optional context for the secondary keywords field.
help.why: Secondary keywords help coverage while staying natural.
help.example: Good example: kitchen drawer organizer, utensil organizer, silverware tray, office drawer organizer
help.avoid: Avoid keyword stuffing, competitor brand terms, or irrelevant search terms.

variables.label: Positioning Angle
variables.placeholder: Select positioning angle
variables.hint: Choose the angle that best fits the product and buyer.

help.what: Optional context for the positioning angle field.
help.why: Positioning controls which benefits receive emphasis.
help.example: Good example: Practical value
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Tone and Style
variables.placeholder: Select tone and style
variables.hint: Choose the writing style for the listing.

help.what: Optional context for the tone and style field.
help.why: Tone affects clarity, confidence, and category fit.
help.example: Good example: Clear and natural
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Required or Avoided Wording
variables.placeholder: Use exact dimensions. Avoid best, guaranteed, indestructible, and eco-friendly.
variables.hint: List phrases to include or avoid.

help.what: Optional context for the required or avoided wording field.
help.why: Wording constraints prevent unsafe claims and preserve brand language.
help.example: Good example: Use exact dimensions. Avoid best, guaranteed, indestructible, and eco-friendly.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Product Risk Type
variables.placeholder: Select product risk type
variables.hint: Choose a risk category only when relevant.

help.what: Optional context for the product risk type field.
help.why: Regulated or safety-sensitive products need stricter claim handling.
help.example: Good example: Standard consumer product
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Claims and Supporting Evidence
variables.placeholder: Food-contact safe finish documented by supplier certificate dated May 2026.
variables.hint: Provide evidence for regulated, performance, safety, or health-related claims.

help.what: Optional context for the claims and supporting evidence field.
help.why: Claims should appear only when supported by supplied evidence.
help.example: Good example: Food-contact safe finish documented by supplier certificate dated May 2026.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported medical, safety, performance, environmental, or compliance claims.

variables.label: Seller Central Category Rules
variables.placeholder: No medical claims, no competitor brand names, no promotional language in title.
variables.hint: Paste relevant category, marketplace, or Seller Central rules.

help.what: Optional context for the seller central category rules field.
help.why: Specific rules take priority over generic listing advice.
help.example: Good example: No medical claims, no competitor brand names, no promotional language in title.
help.avoid: Avoid guessing marketplace rules or treating generic guidance as category-specific policy.

variables.label: Product Has Variations
variables.placeholder: False
variables.hint: Choose whether this product has variations. Choose True or False.

help.what: Choose whether the product is sold as a variation family. Variation details appear only when this is True.
help.why: Variation families need distinct notes for sizes, colors, models, or packs.
help.example: Good example: False
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Variation Details
variables.placeholder: Available in natural bamboo and black finish, small and large sizes.
variables.hint: Describe confirmed variations, differences, and naming rules.

help.what: Optional context for the variation details field.
help.why: Variation details prevent mixing attributes across different SKUs.
help.example: Good example: Available in natural bamboo and black finish, small and large sizes.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Current Listing
variables.placeholder: Paste current title, bullets, description, and backend terms.
variables.hint: Paste existing copy if this is a refresh or optimization.

help.what: Optional context for the current listing field.
help.why: Current copy lets the output improve what exists rather than starting blind.
help.example: Good example: Paste current title, bullets, description, and backend terms.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Product Source Material
variables.placeholder: Product spec sheet, packaging copy, supplier material statement, and approved photos.
variables.hint: Add source notes, spec sheets, packaging copy, or approved references.

help.what: Optional context for the product source material field.
help.why: Source material helps keep the listing anchored to verified facts.
help.example: Good example: Product spec sheet, packaging copy, supplier material statement, and approved photos.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.

variables.label: Additional Context
variables.placeholder: Prioritize clarity over maximum character use.
variables.hint: Add any final constraints or context that does not fit elsewhere.

help.what: Optional context for the additional context field.
help.why: This captures unusual requirements without adding extra prompt variables.
help.example: Good example: Prioritize clarity over maximum character use.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed details, fake proof, review language, urgency, competitor claims, or unsupported superlatives.
